title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天 1 个 podcast + 18 条 X + 1 篇 blog，主轴意外是 Kimi K3。Rauchg 直接承认 K3 在他们家的 web 工程 benchmark 上超过了自家闭源 Fable——这是开源权重第一次在综合工程 benchmark 上压过所有闭源对手，整条「开源追平 frontier」的曲线第一次完整画出来。Madhu Guru 顺势给出企业侧的应对清单；Aditya 已经在行动切走 Fable；Levie 把这条曲线翻译成「成本降一档、场景解一档」的工程语言；Dan 准备 vibe-check、但保持怀疑。另一边，Anthropic 把 agent containment 的工程手册公开，把 blast radius 这件事摆到台面；Boris 接着给出 Claude Code 的 4 级成熟度阶梯。Podcast 这边 Benedict Evans 上 Unsupervised Learning，把「AI 是新电力」这类手挥式类比挡掉，并指出 capex 越大的层 moat 反而越窄。周末的 fragment 是 Sama 说新的语音模型真的跨过了门槛。

archive.title: K3 越线日
archive.desc: 今天 1 podcast + 18 X + 1 blog，主轴是 Kimi K3 在 web 工程 benchmark 上首次超过所有闭源对手——Rauchg 公开承认，Levie 接住「成本降一档场景解一档」，Madhu Guru 给出企业侧应对清单。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-07-18 source feeds.

sections.title: Kimi K3 越线：开源权重在 web 工程评测上首次登顶 / Kimi K3 Crosses the Line: Open Weights Top the Web-Engineering Benchmark
sections.desc: Rauchg 公开承认 K3 在他们自家的 web 评测上超过了自家闭源 Fable——这是开源权重第一次在综合工程基准上压过所有闭源对手。Levie 顺势接住：每次 frontier 智能的成本降一档，企业被 token 价格卡住的 workflow 就会被解锁一档。

rewrite: Kimi K3 just took the top spot on our public web-engineering benchmark — ahead of our own Fable model — and did so in less wall-clock time.

It's the first time an open-weight model has crossed above every proprietary model we test against for this comprehensive web-build benchmark. Benchmarks aren't gospel, but they're a real signal, and the evidence that the open / proprietary gap has collapsed is now mounting.
original: Kimi K3 is the best performing model on our public web-engineering benchmark, ahead of Fable, reaching a comparable success rate in less time.
This is the first time that an open model is ahead of all proprietary ones for this comprehensive web engineering benchmark.
Notes: ▪️ Benchmarks don't always tell the full story, although this is important signal, adding to mounting evidence that this could be a bre

rewrite: Kimi K3 刚刚在我们公开的 web 工程 benchmark 上拿下第一，超过了我们自己的 Fable 模型，而且耗时更短。
这是第一次有一款开源权重模型在这套综合性 web 构建评测里，把所有闭源模型都比下去了。Benchmark 不是圣经，但它是个实在的信号，open / 闭源差距已经塌方的证据正在越积越多。
original: Kimi K3 超越了 Fable，登顶我们公开的 web 工程评测；
这是开源权重模型首次在这套综合 web 工程评测中超过所有闭源模型；
注：Benchmark 不一定讲完整个故事，但是重要的信号，进一步证明了开源与闭源之间的差距正在坍塌。

sections.title: 模型商品化之后：开源倒逼企业栈改设计 / After Model Commoditization: Open Weights Force an Enterprise Stack Rethink
sections.desc: Madhu Guru 直接给出企业侧的 3 步应对——评测分两层、按 query 路由、内部搭一个轻量 gateway。Aditya 已经在行动：把团队的系统从 Fable 切走。Dan 准备 vibe-check K3，并补刀 OpenAI 在 agentic coding 上慢了一拍。

rewrite: Open weights at frontier quality is wild. Every time the cost of frontier intelligence drops, the set of use-cases enterprises will actually deploy goes up — most enterprise workflows are gated by token cost, not capability.

The combined breakthrough of open weights + cheap inference is also a tailwind for the startup ecosystem: when the model layer commoditizes, value migrates up the stack into applications, data, and distribution.
original: It's truly wild that we're getting this level of performance from open models. Congrats to Kimi team on this.

Every time we lower the cost of frontier intelligence, the use-cases that enterprises can take on just go up. There's a tremendous amount of workflows that enterprises would love to deploy that are only gated by the cost of tokens.
Importantly for the startup ecosystem, the combined brea

rewrite: 开源权重跑到 frontier 水平，真的很炸。每次 frontier 智能的成本降一档，企业愿意真金白银上线的新场景就多一档——大多数企业工作流的瓶颈是 token 价格，不是能力上限。
更关键的是，开源权重 + 廉价推理这一对组合，对创业生态是顺风：模型层一旦商品化，价值就会沿着栈往上迁移，落到应用、数据、分发层。
original: 前沿级别的开源模型真的很疯狂，恭喜 Kimi 团队。
我们每降低一次前沿智能的成本，企业可以承担的应用场景就会增加。
对于创业生态而言，这种开源权重与低成本推理的结合是巨大的推动力。

cards.authorTag: AI 工程师 / 开源贡献者

rewrite: Open-weight models like Kimi and GLM are going to force a full rethink of the enterprise AI stack. The move: maximise model optionality.

Three things to actually do — (1) evals split into regression (tablestakes features that must always work) and aspirational (hill-climb toward capability gains); (2) cost-aware routing by query type; (3) ship a small internal gateway so swapping providers is a config change, not a refactor.
original: Open-weight models like Kimi and GLM will cause a complete rethink of the enterprise AI stack.
If you are running an enterprise, you need to maximimize model optionality.

Here are 3 things you should be doing: 1. Evals - build rigorous evals representative of your use case (a) Regression evals - tablestakes features that should always work with high reliability (b) Aspirational / hill-climbing

rewrite: Kimi、GLM 这种开源权重模型，会把企业 AI 整套技术栈逼着重新设计。核心动作就是：把模型可选性（model optionality）拉满。
具体三件事——（1）评测体系要拆成两层：回归评测（必须永远稳的基础功能）+ 爬山评测（向能力上限爬）；（2）按 query 类型做成本感知路由；（3）内部搭一个轻量 gateway，换供应商只是改配置，不是改代码。
original: Kimi 和 GLM 等开源权重模型将彻底颠覆企业级 AI 技术栈。
如果你在运营一家企业，你需要最大化模型的可选择性。
以下是你应该做的 3 件事：1. 构建能代表你用例的严格评测。

cards.authorTag: Dropbox Engineering VP / 前 Facebook 平台工程负责人

rewrite: Aditya is literally switching his team's systems off Fable today — not to be hyperbolic, but if there's a good free alternative, why pay the price?
His rule: if something is so valuable that letting people use it lets them recreate it, maybe it wasn't very valuable in the first place. Frontier labs face this test now.
original: I am literally switching models off of Fable right now for our systems.
This isn't trying to be hyperbolic....but why would you pay the price if there is a good and free alternative?
If you have something very valuable....but letting people use it allows them to recreate it....then maybe it wasn't very valuable in the first place?

rewrite: Aditya 今天就在把自己团队的系统从 Fable 切走——不是夸张，而是只要有靠谱的免费替代，为什么要付那个钱？
他的判断标准是：一样东西如果「让人用了就能复刻」还照样有价值，那它本身可能就没那么有价值。Frontier 实验室现在就在被这道题考。
original: 我现在正在把系统中的模型从 Fable 切走。
这不是为了夸大其词……但如果有不错的免费替代品，为什么要付费？
如果你拥有的东西非常有价值……但让别人使用它就能复制它……那它一开始可能就没那么有价值。

cards.authorTag: Every 联合创始人 / AI 写作工具链

rewrite: Dan will vibe-check Kimi K3 — but he's extraordinarily skeptical of claims it's as good as Fable. The vibes-vs-receipts test matters more than ever this week.

His read on OpenAI: not surprising they're firing on all cylinders; GPT-5 was positioned as a pair-programmer but missed the agentic-coding wave that Claude Code started; they're now back-footed in agentic coding in the browser / VMs and vibe coding in ChatGPT, and need to re-converge.
original: we will vibe check Kimi K3 but i am extraordinarily skeptical of claims it's as good as fable

not surprising that @OpenAI is firing on all cylinders right now, and it's an unbelievably interesting story - they launched GPT-5 summer of 2025 and positioned it as a pair programmer. we wrote at the time @every that they completely missed the new agentic coding that was starting to happen inside of Claude Code. they bet on agentic coding in the browser / vms and vibe coding in ChatGPT but it w
look at those emoji reactions. this is how we win

rewrite: Dan 要对 Kimi K3 做 vibe-check，但他对「K3 比肩 Fable」这种说法保持高度怀疑。这周，vibes 和 receipts 的检验比任何时候都关键。
他对 OpenAI 的判断是：现在全力开火并不奇怪；GPT-5 当时定位成 pair-programmer，错过了 Claude Code 掀起的 agentic coding 浪潮；他们在 browser / VM 里的 agentic coding 和 ChatGPT 的 vibe coding 上都慢了一步，需要重新对齐。
original: 我们要 vibe-check Kimi K3，但我对「它和 Fable 一样好」这种说法持极端怀疑态度。
OpenAI 现在全力开火并不令人意外，故事非常有意思——他们在 2025 年夏天推出 GPT-5，把它定位为结对程序员。

sections.title: 把 Claude 关进笼子：Anthropic 的 blast-radius 工程手册 / Containing Claude: Anthropic's Blast-Radius Engineering Playbook
sections.desc: Anthropic 官博首次系统讲清怎么给 agent 越来越大的权限——核心是把 blast radius 卡死，不是把概率降零。Boris Cherny 接着给出一份 4 级成熟度阶梯，Anthropic 自己在 3 级往 4 级推。

rewrite: Anthropic just published the engineering playbook for containing Claude as agents get more autonomy. The thesis: 12 months ago they'd have rejected any deployment where Claude had access to take down an internal service; today that's routine, because the productivity win is too large to leave on the table.

Two failure modes to manage: probability of failure (driven by safeguards + training) and blast radius (which only grows as capabilities expand). The engineering problem becomes capping the blast radius — sandbox the environment, constrain the agent's tools, and put hard ceiling on what it can mutate.
original: Twelve months ago, we'd have rejected out of hand the idea of granting Claude access sufficient to take down an internal Anthropic service. Today that level of access is routine, and Anthropic developers are more productive for it.
The risk of these deployments has two components: how likely a failure is, and how much damage one could do.
Progress on safeguards and model training has steadily driven down the first; the second—the theoretical blast radius—only grows as capabilities and access expand.
Yet as agents become capable of doing work that once required a person or even a team, the cost of not deploying grows large enough that the risk-reward calculation tips heavily toward adoption

rewrite: Anthropic 刚发了一份工程实战手册，讲怎么在 agent 越来越自主的同时把 Claude 关进笼子。核心论点：12 个月前，他们会把「让 Claude 有权关掉 Anthropic 内部服务」这种部署一口回绝；今天这已经是常态，因为生产力的诱惑大到不能放着不用。
要管两件事：失败的概率（靠 safeguards 和训练往下压），以及失败时的 blast radius（随着能力扩大只会涨不会缩）。工程难题变成：把 blast radius 卡死——沙箱环境、约束工具、对 agent 能改的东西划硬上限。
original: 一年前，我们会毫不犹豫地拒绝授予 Claude 足以关停 Anthropic 内部服务的访问权限。如今，这种访问权限已是常态，Anthropic 开发者也因此更具生产力。
这类部署的风险由两部分组成：失败的可能性，以及一次失败可能造成的损害。

rewrite: Boris lays out the 4-step adoption ladder Anthropic engineers move through with Claude Code. The bigger payoff lands when fixing and maintaining happens in the background and your team can focus on building — that's when you start doing things that weren't even in range before.
His team is at step 4; he's curious where other teams are. Step 1 = chat; step 2 = inline completion; step 3 = background agents; step 4 = multi-agent orchestration with auto-review + verification.
original: The bigger payoff comes when fixing and maintaining happens in the background and your teams can focus on building. That's when you start doing things that weren't even in range before.
Anthropic is on step 3 and pushing toward 4. Personally, I just hit level 4.
Curious where you are -- what step is your team on?

rewrite: Boris 把团队用 Claude Code 的成熟度拆成 4 级。真正的回报发生在「修复和维护都在后台跑，团队专心做构建」的时候——到这一步，你才能开始做以前够不着的事。
Anthropic 自己在第 3 级，正在往 4 级推；他个人刚到 4 级。他好奇别的团队在哪一级：1 级=聊天；2 级=内联补全；3 级=后台 agent；4 级=多 agent 编排，带自动 review 和验证。
original: 真正的回报来自于修复和维护工作在后台完成，你的团队可以专注于构建。那时你就能开始做一些以前甚至做不到的事情。
Anthropic 目前在第 3 步，正向第 4 步迈进。就我个人而言，刚刚达到第 4 级。

rewrite: On measuring the adoption curve: usage dashboards measure activity, not return. The sharper question is — would you have spent engineering effort on this anyway? If yes, how much, and what would it have cost in manual eng-hours? That delta is the return.

On the practical side: give Claude ways to verify its own work end to end, default on automated code review + security review, use multi-agent interfaces (Agent view in CLI, Desktop, mobile, Tag), and reach for /loop, /batch, worktree isolation for higher steps.
original: Once your teams are bought in, how do you track it? Usage is worth watching (e.g. a dashboard), but it measures activity, not return. A better question: would you have spent engineering effort on this anyway? If yes, how much and what would it have cost in manual eng-hours? That's your return.

In practice that means giving Claude ways to verify its own work end to end. It means enabling auto mode for permissions, defaulting on automated code review and security review, and using interfaces that let you manage multiple agents at once (Agent view in CLI, Desktop app, iOS and Android apps, Tag). To get to higher levels it means /loop, /batch, dynamic workflows, and worktree isolation for

rewrite: 怎么衡量 adoption 的回报？使用量仪表盘衡量的是「活动量」，不是「回报」。更锋利的问题是：这件事如果你不用 Claude，本来也要投入工程力去做吗？如果是，量多大、折算成多少手动工程工时？这个差值才是回报。
落到实践上：让 Claude 能端到端自验、默认开启自动 code review + security review、用多 agent 界面（CLI 的 Agent view、Desktop、移动端、Tag）；再往上靠 /loop、/batch、worktree 隔离。
original: 一旦团队接受了，你怎么跟踪？使用情况值得观察，但衡量的是活动而非回报。一个更好的问题是：你本来也会在工程上花力气做这件事吗？

sections.title: Benedict Evans：别把 AI 当电力，先看清哪里有 moat / Benedict Evans: Don't Call AI Electricity; Look for the Moat
sections.desc: Unsupervised Learning 第 91 期请来 Benedict Evans。两条主线：Hinton 十年前的「别再训练放射科医生」是「聪明人不理解 workflow」的样本；capex 越大、moat 反而越窄，真正值得打的仗在应用层和切换成本。

cards.authorTag: Redpoint 出品的 AI 投资与技术播客

rewrite: Benedict Evans pushes back on the 'AI is like electricity' hand-wave. The deeper test: what happened with electricity? People before electrification weren't dumb either. The real question is which industries get restructured first, and which resist.

On the job-loss panic: Hinton's 10-year-old claim that we should stop training radiologists is the canonical example of smart people misunderstanding what those jobs actually involve. The pattern repeats with every new model release — pattern-match a headline job title, declare it dead, ignore the actual workflow.
original: You can wave your hands and say, no. This is like electricity. Okay. Fine. It's like electricity. Well, what happened with electricity? And there was a time before electricity, and people before electricity weren't dumb either.

We get deep into logical fallacies here. So we've got Jeff Hinton, was ten years ago, saying stop training radiologists. And the problem here here is he doesn't actually understand what radiologists do.

rewrite: Benedict Evans 反驳「AI 就是电力」这种挥手式类比。更扎实的测试是：电力当年到底发生了什么？电气化之前的人也不傻。真正的问题是哪些行业先被重塑、哪些行业会抵抗。
对于「AI 取代工作」的恐慌：Hinton 十年前说别再训练放射科医生，就是聪明人不理解那份工作到底在干嘛的典型样本。每次新模型发布，这个模式都重演——抓一个吸睛的岗位名，宣布它已死，忽略真实的工作流。
original: 你可以挥挥手说，不，这就像电力。好吧。细想一下电力时代发生了什么？电气化之前的人也不傻。
我们在这里深入探讨逻辑谬误。十年前 Hinton 说过停止训练放射科医生。这里的问题是他实际上并不理解放射科医生是做什么的。

cards.authorTag: Redpoint 出品的 AI 投资与技术播客

rewrite: On where value accrues: foundation-model valuations are a separate question from where the actual economic value will land. Evans' heuristic — the bigger the capex required to build the thing, the narrower the eventual moat, and the more value gets competed away downstream.

His closing implication for builders: don't optimize for the foundation-model layer. The interesting fight is in the application surface that sits on top — and on whether you can build a workflow so embedded that switching costs become real.
original: Speaker 2 | 00:28 - 00:46: I'm Jacob Efron from Redpoint, and this is Unsupervised Learning. A podcast where we probe the sharpest minds in AI about what's happening in the ecosystem today and where the future's headed.
Speaker 2 | 00:46 - 01:10: We talked about where value will accrue in AI, as well as what he thinks the ultimate valuations of the foundation models might look like. We talked about why consumer ...

rewrite: 关于价值归向：基础模型的估值和「真实经济价值最终落在哪」是两个问题。Evans 的启发式判断是——构建所需 capex 越大，最终的护城河反而越窄，下游被竞争摊薄的部分越多。
他留给 builder 的隐含建议：别在基础模型层做文章。真正的仗在它上面的应用层——能不能把 workflow 嵌入得够深，让切换成本变成现实。
original: 我是来自红点的 Jacob Efron，这是《无监督学习》。
我们讨论了 AI 的价值将如何积累，以及他对基础模型最终估值的看法。

sections.title: NotebookLM 毕业：Labs 那个小实验今天叫 Gemini Notebook / NotebookLM Graduates: The Labs Experiment Is Now Gemini Notebook
sections.desc: Josh Woodward 亲历回溯：当年那个看 Steven Johnson 拆书写作的小房间是起点，今天 3000 万+ 用户、60 万+ 组织。Google Labs 补刀欢迎它升级到 Gemini Notebook 品牌。

rewrite: NotebookLM is graduating out of Labs. Josh still remembers the room where @stevenbjohnson walked through how he writes books — that was the spark for a small project nobody imagined would grow this big. Today: 30M+ people, 600K+ organizations.
The internal name was just 'Notebook' for years. Today it's official externally: Gemini Notebook.
original: I still remember the exact room, watching @stevenbjohnson break down how he writes books. That was the spark for a little project we never imagined would grow this big.
Today, over 30 million people and 600,000 organizations use it, and it keeps growing. We all feel like it's just getting started.
For a while, we've just called it "Notebook" internally. Today, we're making it official externally

rewrite: NotebookLM 从 Labs 正式「毕业」。Josh 还记得那个房间——看着 @stevenbjohnson 拆解他是怎么写书的——那是一颗谁都没想到会长这么大的小种子。
今天：3000 万+ 用户，60 万+ 组织。内部一直就叫「Notebook」。今天对外正式定名：Gemini Notebook。
original: 我还记得那个房间，看着 @stevenbjohnson 拆解他是怎么写书的。那是一个没人想到会长这么大的小项目的火花。
如今，超过 3000 万人和 60 万个组织在使用它，而且还在不断增长。

rewrite: From Google Labs: who remembers Project Tailwind — the little baby Labs experiment? It became NotebookLM. Today it becomes @Gemini_Notebook — congrats to the team on the rebrand.
original: Who remembers when Project Tailwind was just a little, baby Labs experiment? Well, eventually that became NotebookLM. And today - many congrats to the team on becoming @Gemini_Notebook!

rewrite: Google Labs 官方补刀：还记得当年那个叫 Project Tailwind 的小实验吗？后来变成 NotebookLM。今天再升级成 @Gemini_Notebook，恭喜团队完成品牌升级。
original: 谁还记得当年 Project Tailwind 还只是一个小小的实验室实验？最终它变成了 NotebookLM。今天——热烈祝贺团队升级为 @Gemini_Notebook！

sections.title: 周末 fragment 三连：Sama 跨过语音门槛 + Amjad 把 RL 模板公开 + Thibault 修桌面端 / Weekend Fragments: Sama Crosses the Voice Threshold + Amjad Open-Sources the RL Template + Thibault Fixes the Desktop
sections.desc: Sama 说新语音模型真的「跨过了门槛」，并预告接下来 12 个月是 OpenAI 史上最强。Amjad 把 2M Stockfish 局面 + 短程 GRPO 的国际象棋引擎全部公开。Thibault 列出 ChatGPT 桌面端的修正清单。

rewrite: Sama on the new ChatGPT voice model: 'I talk to ChatGPT more than I type to it at this point — the new voice model really crossed a threshold.' On OpenAI's roadmap: 'we didn't have our best last 12 months — that's mostly my fault — but we're about to have our best 12 months to date.'
original: i talk to chatgpt more than i type to it at this point. new voice model really crossed a threshold

we did not have our best last 12 months ever, which is mostly my fault, but we are about to have our best 12 months to date. the team is doing amazing work and i think you'll be very happy with what they've got cooking for you. i am happy about this for many reasons, but mostly because i care about our users winning. AI has to be about giving lots of people more freedom, agency, and wealth. we wa

rewrite: Sama 谈新的 ChatGPT 语音模型：「我现在跟 ChatGPT 说话比打字还多——新的语音模型真的跨过了某个门槛。」他接着说 OpenAI 的路线图：「过去 12 个月不是我们最好的 12 个月，主要是我自己的锅；但接下来的 12 个月会是我们史上最好的 12 个月。」
original: 现在我与 ChatGPT 的交流中，说话多于打字。新的语音模型真的跨越了一个临界点。
过去 12 个月不是我们表现最好的时期，这主要是我自己的责任，但接下来的 12 个月将是我们迄今为止最好的 12 个月。

rewrite: Amjad ships a Replit-side chess engine that already beats frontier models on the board. The recipe: fine-tune on 2M Stockfish-labeled positions, then a short GRPO RL pass. Annotated code + experiments + tutorial are public — a clean template for small-model-on-narrow-task RL.
Side note on NVDA: Kimi K3 is news and the stock should be pumping; instead it's down. Distillation paradox — a model can outperform its teacher.
original: $NVDA should be pumping on the K3 news. Instead it's down.
Apparently the distillation model can outperform the teacher model

If you want to play my chess engine (WIP): https://... It already seems to perform better than frontier models on chess. It's fine-tuned on 2M stockfish-labeled positions, then short GRPO RL pass. Documentation/tutorial with all the experiments and annotated code.

rewrite: Amjad 在 Replit 上放出自己的国际象棋引擎，已经能正面击败 frontier 模型。配方：200 万 Stockfish 标注局面做微调，再来一轮短程 GRPO RL。代码、实验、教程全部公开——一份干净的小模型 + 窄任务 RL 模板。
顺便提一句 NVDA：Kimi K3 是利好，股价本该涨，结果反而跌。蒸馏悖论——学生可以反过来超过老师。
original: NVDA 应该因为 K3 的消息上涨，结果反而下跌了。
显然，蒸馏模型可以表现得比它的老师更好。

rewrite: Thibault details the ChatGPT desktop-app fix list: conversation history + projects now in the sidebar; chat/work history syncs across web/mobile/desktop (local tasks stay on the device); easier model-switching; better handoff with Codex. Acknowledges the v1 didn't nail it — the new release is the corrected shape.
original: Evening! We've gotten lots of great feedback on the new ChatGPT desktop app (which we didn't get totally quite right on the first try), and as a result, we've made some changes. 1/ ChatGPT conversation history and projects are now visible in the sidebar. Also, your Chat and Work history now sync across web, mobile, and desktop. Local tasks still stay on your computer. 2/ You can now easily switch

rewrite: Thibault 列出 ChatGPT 桌面端应用的修复清单：会话历史 + 项目现在进侧边栏；聊天/工作历史在 web / mobile / desktop 之间同步（本地任务仍留在本机）；模型切换更顺；和 Codex 的衔接也更好。他承认 v1 没做到位——这次发的是修正后的形态。
original: 晚上好！关于新的 ChatGPT 桌面应用，我们收到了很多很好的反馈（我们第一次没有完全做对），因此我们做出了一些更改。
